product_overview: Yourself is a single‑user productivity app that helps people structure goals across time horizons, run focused sessions, and reflect with journals and failure logs. It combines local offline state with optional authenticated backend sync and supports exports, trash/restore, and structured progress rollups.
core_goals: Capture and organize goals across time horizons (year → month → week → day) including parent/child relationships and plans
Run and record timed focus sessions and lighter daily simple sessions tied to goals
Maintain daily and goal-linked journals for reflection and learning
Track and review explicit failures and recover deleted items from a Trash area
Export goal summaries and full backups and perform a deliberate system wipe when desired

features.name: Dashboard (overview & trends)
features.description: At-a-glance performance overview showing progress rollups and visual trends by goal type and life area, computed in the user's timezone.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> See dashboard overview with aggregates and charts for goals and timeframes
Navigate to / -> Filter or inspect a timeframe -> Observe updated aggregates and chart trends

features.name: Goals and sub-goals
features.description: Create, edit, organize and plan goals across types (yearly/monthly/weekly/daily), attach frameworks, and spawn child goals from plans.
user_flows: Navigate to /goals -> Create a new goal with type, title, and optional plan/framework -> Save goal -> See the new goal in the list
Navigate to /goals -> Open an existing goal -> Edit goal fields -> Save changes -> See updated goal in list
Navigate to /goals -> Create a plan for a parent goal -> Spawn child goals from the plan -> See spawned child goals added to the hierarchy
Navigate to /goals -> Attempt to save a goal without a required field (e.g. missing title) -> See validation preventing save and an explanation of the missing input

features.name: Sessions (timed focus & daily simple sessions)
features.description: Start, stop, and record timed focus sessions (with work/rest durations and framework fields) and manage lightweight daily simple sessions for daily goals.
user_flows: Navigate to /session -> Start a timed focus session -> Complete the session -> Mark whether the goal was achieved and enter optional notes -> Save session -> See session recorded for the goal
Navigate to /session -> Start a timed focus session -> End the session early and mark as skipped with a reason -> Save session -> See session recorded as skipped
Navigate to /goals -> Select a daily goal -> Add a daily simple session -> Mark the daily simple session done -> See the daily simple session status updated
Navigate to /goals -> Select a daily goal -> Add a daily simple session -> Mark the daily simple session missed -> Save note -> See the daily simple session status updated

features.name: Journal (life & goal-linked)
features.description: Write daily life journal entries and goal-linked journal Q&A entries filtered by timeframe and goal type; saves only when content is provided.
user_flows: Navigate to /journal -> Open Daily tab -> Enter content into at least one life journal field -> Save entry -> See saved life journal for the date
Navigate to /journal -> Open Goals tab -> Select a timeframe and a goal -> Answer goal journal questions -> Save journal answers -> See journal entries linked to the goal and date
Navigate to /journal -> Open Daily tab -> Attempt to save with all life journal fields empty -> See validation preventing save and instruction to add content

features.name: Failures (explicit setback logging)
features.description: Log, edit, and remove explicit failure entries that are attached to a session or a goal, with a required note for reflection.
user_flows: Navigate to /failures -> Create a new failure -> Choose whether it relates to a session or a goal and select the relevant item -> Enter a required note describing the failure -> Save failure -> See the failure appear in the list with context
Navigate to /failures -> Open an existing failure -> Edit the note or linked context -> Save changes -> See the updated failure in the list
Navigate to /failures -> Attempt to save a failure without a note -> See validation preventing save and an explanation that a note is required

features.name: Trash (soft-delete, restore, purge)
features.description: Recover or permanently delete soft-deleted items; provide strong confirmations for irreversible purge actions.
user_flows: Navigate to /trash -> Browse deleted items grouped by type -> Select an item to restore -> Confirm restore -> See the item returned to its active location
Navigate to /trash -> Select one or more items -> Choose purge permanently -> Confirm purge with explicit acceptance -> See items removed from trash

features.name: Export and data control (PDF summary, full backup, system wipe)
features.description: Export a PDF summary for a selected goal, download a full JSON backup, and perform a deliberate system wipe that clears local and backend data after confirmation.
user_flows: Open the layout export controls (sidebar) -> Choose Export Summary (PDF) -> Select the goal to export -> Generate and download the PDF summary for that goal
Open the layout export controls (sidebar) -> Choose Export Full Backup -> Confirm export -> Download a JSON backup of user data
Open the layout export controls (sidebar) -> Choose System Wipe -> Read the confirmation instructions -> Confirm the wipe explicitly -> See the app clear local data and reload

features.name: Authentication (email/password)
features.description: Email/password sign-in when authentication is enabled; when auth is disabled in the environment the app skips login and proceeds to the dashboard.
user_flows: Navigate to /login -> Enter email -> Enter password -> Submit login credentials -> See the dashboard after successful sign‑in
Navigate to /login when authentication is disabled via configuration -> Be redirected to / -> See the dashboard without signing in

routes.description: Firebase email/password login; skipped when VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=false (redirects to /)
Session timer and daily simple sessions

features.name: Goals and sub-goals
features.description: CRUD goals, frameworks, plan PDF export, local Dexie sync with backend API when authenticated

features.name: Sessions
features.description: Timer sessions and daily simple session streaks
user_interactions: Mark daily session done or missed

known_limitations.issue: Production Vercel build may use VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=true; local testsprite uses VITE_AUTH_ENABLED=false via dev:testsprite
known_limitations.impact: E2E should use hash routes without login when auth is disabled
